content:
I get this:

rust/kernel.o: error: objtool: _RNvXNtNtCsaRPFapPOzLs_6kernel3str9parse_intaNtNtB2_7private12FromStrRadix14from_str_radix() falls through to next function _RNvXNtNtCsaRPFapPOzLs_6kernel3str9parse_intaNtNtB2_7private12FromStrRadix16from_u64_negated()

it seems to be the first fallthrough case outside of core
oh god it only falls through on some rustc versions, so adding it to the list causes failures on other rustc versions
I think we ought to disable this check for Rust altogether. Objtool has a flag for this although I haven't figured out how to selectively enable it for Rust only from Makefile.

it seems to be the first fallthrough case outside of core

This was fixed in commit c18f35e49049 ("objtool/rust: add one more noreturn Rust function"). Are you using something older than v6.19-rc8?

I think we ought to disable this check for Rust altogether.

It would make my life easier, and I agree it wouldn't be a big risk, but if possible we should try our best to avoid disabling checks that C has.

For instance, if we get the list of symbols from upstream rustc, then we could disable it in older versions, for instance.

Miguel Ojeda said:

it seems to be the first fallthrough case outside of core

This was fixed in commit c18f35e49049 ("objtool/rust: add one more noreturn Rust function"). Are you using something older than v6.19-rc8?

I ran it checked out to exactly that commit:
https://github.com/Darksonn/linux/actions/runs/21755770700/job/62765485681?pr=2
Then it is not coming from from_ascii_radix_panic but another one. I will try to reproduce it.
yeah this one is from_str_radix()
No, the commit also fixed one from from_str_radix, but what it means is that we are missing yet another annotation if it can still be triggered.

Your config says:

# Linux/x86_64 6.19.0-rc4 Kernel Configuration
commit c18f35e49049 is based on v6.19-rc4, so that seems consistentn
Ah, you mean you checked out on the fix, not the tag, got it.

Alice Ryhl said:

oh god it only falls through on some rustc versions, so adding it to the list causes failures on other rustc versions

What did you add to the list?
str_ends_with(func->name, "kernel3str9parse_intaNtNtB2_7private12FromStrRadix14from_str_radix")
But that isn't noreturn
the error message claims it falls through, tho

diff --git a/tools/objtool/check.c b/tools/objtool/check.c
index 719ec727efd48..7cfb8c8f2fab8 100644
--- a/tools/objtool/check.c
+++ b/tools/objtool/check.c
@@ -197,7 +197,8 @@ static bool is_rust_noreturn(const struct symbol *func)
         * as well as changes to the source code itself between versions (since
         * these come from the Rust standard library).
         */
-       return str_ends_with(func->name, "_4core3num22from_ascii_radix_panic")                          ||
+       return str_ends_with(func->name, "_4core3num20from_str_radix_panic")                            ||
+              str_ends_with(func->name, "_4core3num22from_ascii_radix_panic")                          ||
               str_ends_with(func->name, "_4core5sliceSp15copy_from_slice17len_mismatch_fail")          ||
               str_ends_with(func->name, "_4core6option13expect_failed")                                ||
               str_ends_with(func->name, "_4core6option13unwrap_failed")                                ||

Alice Ryhl said:

the error message claims it falls through, tho

Yes, but that is because objtool does not know what functions are actually noreturn
I quickly tested the one above with Rust stable and 1.84.0 so far

Miguel Ojeda said:

Yes, but that is because objtool does not know what functions are actually noreturn

i.e. objtool thinks it fallsthrough, but it isn't true, and it thinks so because it doesn't have enough information about what is noreturn
The list is about giving objtool enough information to know what is going on
ok I see
It is a hack, to be clear, so that is why I want upstream rustc to add the flag to give us the information
and Gary has other suggestions, like using debug information, but that only works sometimes
We could disable this entirely, and only do the analysis when we don't have debug information -- that would also be fair
Ok, I will test the diff above and send a patch later
Tested-by alice
Thanks!
